mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated IBM Cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM's infrastructure and potential costs associated with managed services.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
The IBM Cloud CLI is required to interact with the IBM Cloud services, including creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command installs the IBM Cloud CLI on your system.

steps.title: Login to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate your IBM Cloud account to access the Kubernetes service.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> Follow the prompts to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-nodes 3

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your kubeconfig file to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command sets the context for kubectl.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Consider setting up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/IBM-Cloud/kube-samples/main/monitoring.yaml

```

> This example sets up monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Data loss is permanent.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> This will delete the cluster and all resources associated with it.

uninstall.title: Cleanup Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Ensure that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are also removed.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete my-load-balancer --force

```

> Replace with your actual load balancer name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your applications and configurations. Then, upgrade the cluster version.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

```

> This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

upgrade.title: Verify Upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the nodes after the upgrade to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Ensure all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

upgrade.title: Rollback if Necessary
upgrade.description:
If issues arise, you can rollback to the previous version using the cluster-update command.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.29

```

> Replace with the previous version if needed.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to set the kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available capacity in the selected zone.

**Fix:**
Try reducing the number of nodes or selecting a different zone.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is still initializing or has failed.

**Fix:**
Check the node status with 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that kubectl is not installed or not in your PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl by following the installation instructions for your OS.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved scheduling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy authentication methods.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure all applications are compatible with the new PodSecurity standards.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' with all nodes in 'Ready' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed before proceeding.
